title_en: Batman/Superman/Weird Al: World’s Weirdest Is the Superhero Crossover We Didn't Know We Needed
summary_en: The Dark Knight and the Man of Steel are gaining a powerful new ally, as DC reveals the epic crossover Batman/Superman/Weird Al: World’s Weirdest.
body_en:
When Batman and Superman join forces, you have one of the mightiest pairs in pop culture. It seems almost unfair to throw "Weird Al" Yankovic into that mix, but that's exactly what DC is doing with a new crossover comic called Batman/Superman/Weird Al: World’s Weirdest.
Batman/Superman/Weird Al: World’s Weirdest brings these three heroic icons together for a one-shot adventure that spans the Fifth Dimension. Check out the slideshow gallery below to see cover art for this issue:
Batman/Superman/Weird Al: World’s Weirdest #1 is written by Batman/Superman: World's Finest's Mark Waid and drawn by features interior art and a main cover by Dan Schoening (Ghostbusters). DC teases that Yankovic himself contributes "vibes" to this issue, so take that for what you will. Here's the official summary for Batman/Superman/Weird Al: World’s Weirdest #1:
Weird Al fans may remember that this isn't the first time he's crossed paths with the Caped Crusader. The iconic parody singer previously guest-starred in an episode of Batman: The Brave and the Bold, one which also featured Scooby-Doo and the Mystery Inc. gang.
Batman/Superman/Weird Al: World’s Weirdest #1 will be released on September 2. You can preorder a copy at your local comic shop.
For more, check out Marvel's newest Avengers roster and see an exclusive preview of Tom Morello's latest comic book .
Jesse is a mild-mannered staff writer for IGN. Allow him to lend a machete to your intellectual thicket by following @jschedeen on BlueSky .
